rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. DANIEL Y WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Null And Void The Abstract Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 1. PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S Motion To Null And Void The Abstract Of Judgment. Denied. As to the proof of service, moving party has not served Jeffrey Peter Woo, who according to the Court's records is counsel of record for the defendant. Even if Daniel Wong individually is the proper person to serve, the address at which he was served does not match the address on the abstract of judgment. Further, moving party has not provided any legal citations or analysis for the relief requested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE LEE VS. 227 9TH STREET APARTMENTS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition Of Def Cynthia Resma And Richard Resma And Request Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JAMIE LEE Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition Of Def Cynthia Resma And Richard Resma And Request Produce Documents Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Hearing required to set date for depositions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES LLC AS ASSIGNEE OF VS. AFTAB CHOUDHARY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing To Approve Stipulation For Judgment; Ntc Of Submission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 4. PLAINTIFF CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES LLC'S Hearing To Approve Stipulation For Judgment. Denied, without prejudice. Plaintiff did not file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of its motion. (CRC 3.1112(a)(3); 3.1113(a).) This motion does not fall within one of the exceptions to filing a memorandum. (CRC 3.1114(a).) Plaintiff has not identified the statutory grounds for entry of judgment. rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LEE VS. MOROSO CONSTRUCTION INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CHARLES LEE, ALEXANDRA BOYLE Mtn For Award Of Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. It has not been proven to the court that Moroso acted in intentional bad faith as opposed to simply making good faith mistakes in a case involving thousands of pages of documents. That being said Moroso is admonished not to continue making these types of mistakes, and is further admonished that further failure to timely and fully comply with discovery obligations, and further inaccurate representations regarding document production or other discovery matters, may be grounds for stringent sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARIA BUZON et al VS. ALEXANDER ROBERT VARTAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER VARTAN Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery; Req. For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The motion to compel is granted as to plaintiff Lee Buzon. A hearing is required to ascertain when Lee Buzon will be able to comply. The motion to compel is denied as to plaintiff Rosa Buzon as moot since she has now responded. Sanctions are granted against both plaintiffs and their attorney in the amount of $572. A hearing is required as to when the sanctions will be paid. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: BELLAIRE TOWER HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. GOLDEN STATE BROADCASTING LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One, Propounded To Bellevue Homeowners Association, Inc; And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE BROADCASTING, LLC Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One, Propounded To Bellevue Homeowners Association, Inc; And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. All documents and responses that are ordered to be provided shall be provided by September 30. Sanctions are denied. Request 3. Granted in part. Plaintiff shall amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce final operating budgets for each year from 2005 to the present. Plaintiff may redact from the final operating budgets all information that does not show or include incoming revenues from the rooftop transmitter and antenna or the outgoing expenditures on electricity and other utilities. Request 4. Denied. Plaintiff states that there is only one nonprivileged document responsive to this request and that it would be unduly burdensome to find the document. Request 10: Granted in part. Plaintiff shall amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce all PG&E invoices, bills, statements or other DOCUMENTS reflecting or RELATING to the utility expenses charged to any licensee of THE TOWER from 1999 through the present. Request 11: Denied. Plaintiff states that it has no responsive documents. Request 12: Granted in part. Plaintiff shall amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce all license agreements between it and any party licensing any part of THE TOWER for the operation of of a radio station or for any other communication service from the original installation of THE TOWER to the present. Request 13: Denied. Complying would be unduly burdensome and oppressive. Request 14: Denied. There are no nonprivileged documents responsive to this request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ABREHA KIDANE VS. ELMER TEP CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Assignee The National Collection Agency, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Post Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 21. Assignee The National Collection Agency, Inc.'s Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Post Undertaking. Granted. No opposition was filed. On May 25, 2013, Mr. Kidane timely filed with the Court a Notice of Appeal. "As a condition to filing an appeal pursuant to this section, an employer shall first post an undertaking with the reviewing court in the amount of the order, decision, or award. The undertaking shall consist of an appeal bond issued by a licensed surety or a cash deposit with the court in the amount of the order, decision, or award." (Lab. Code § 98.2(b).) On June 13, 2013, Mr. Kidane filed a motion to waive undertaking (bond or cash) requirement of Labor Code § 98.2(b), and on July 10, 2013, the Court denied Mr. Kidane's motion. Mr. Kidane has not filed an undertaking and has not opposed this motion. Therefore, this action is dismissed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK MCGOVERN VS. PATRICK DANIEL POWER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2013, Line 17. DEFENDANT PATRICK POWER'S Amended Motion To Stay Action. Continued to September 13, 2013 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 29, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied]. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f).The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
